author: 𝐑𝐎𝐌𝐄 | Virtuwul [ROME]
content: It sounds like people are taking the excerpts of that comment and looking way too deep into it. First I have assets in both projects and follow Illuvium secondarily only to Star Atlas. That’s two projects have been the gold standard for how dev teams should treat and respond to their communities. Kieran is the bombastic marketing part of the team who makes outlandish claims. His job is to get people talking about Illuvium and remember “there is no such thing as bad press.” Plus his comment is about Star Atlas is not unfounded. That is exactly the talking point that SA must prove wrong for the next 2 to 3 years. I believe SA will blow expectations out of the water, but if comments like Kieran’s are getting under your skin then it’s going to be a long few years, and he has achieved his purpose for boasting. Simple as that.

author: iG [NT]
content: It sounds like people are taking the excerpts of that comment and looking way too deep into it. First I have assets in both projects and follow Illuvium secondarily only to Star Atlas. That’s two projects have been the gold standard for how dev teams should treat and respond to their communities. Kieran is the bombastic marketing part of the team who makes outlandish claims. His job is to get people talking about Illuvium and remember “there is no such thing as bad press.” Plus his comment is about Star Atlas is not unfounded. That is exactly the talking point that SA must prove wrong for the next 2 to 3 years. I believe SA will blow expectations out of the water, but if comments like Kieran’s are getting under your skin then it’s going to be a long few years, and he has achieved his purpose for boasting. Simple as that.

author: TechNoirMUD
content: @ROME | Kalvin Kidean Re your question in FR, the issue with Manus Ultimus Divinum is not with manus being a 4th declension noun (n. manus, g. manūs), but with it being feminine. So at the very least it should be Manus Ultima (see manus dextera, or manus dei). As for Divinum, its meaning here is just obscure: being inflected as neutral form (I guess as nominative), it does not agree with neither manus nor ultimus. I'd go for Manus Ultima Divina if the intended meaning is that on the website, but it sounds artificial to me.

author: Support Line | Star Atlas Mod [STAR]
content: Yeah I agree. Stellaris is more complex than it needs to be. I guess hardcore elites look for such capabilities in a game but most people do not want extraordinary complexity. We need to have the right balance of complexity without deterring most gamers. Having a grand space strategy, FPS and space combat/exploration plus possible VR capabilities are enough to provide a very entertaining and engaging experience. It does not need to be highly complex. Have a solid economy model with P2E is going to provide the remaining requirements and we have a winner on our hands. Please do not try to do too much beyond this. This is more than enough to target for next 426 years.

author: Signal
content: Been tracking your chats here and in FR, intersting topic.  The reason why UBI in blockchain P2E won't necessarily work is because game/tech being global but there is geographical imbalance.  Min Wage USA ~ USD $7/hour Min Wage Australia (where Im from) ~ USD $12/hour  AVERAGE Wage Philipines ~USD $1.38/hour  (meaning a whole shitlaod of ppl get much less than that, assuming 40 hours per week worked on the average yearly salary // there arent many min.wage places in ASEAN)  Which also explains why Axie was absolutely dominated by ASEAN scholars.

author: [AEP] MoonMonger
content:
On the topic of C9 vs Ballad. I think they should have the exact same difficulty of manufacturing because both are from main faction-specific manufacturers, Pearce and Vzus respectively. There should be no difference in acquiring the materials and building as long as you are a MUD building a C9 or an Ustur building a VZUS.

Who wins heads up is a toss-up, I think. There should be a baseline firepower boost to the C9 and Ballad should get a hull strength boost and maneuverability. I think VWAP is normally the best determinant of battle outcome we have atm when comparing fleets, but Ballad has a bit of the GAO2 inflation to discount. There could be other non-combat benefits as well driving the VWAP difference. Future value C9 will be worth more for a long while still until players can actually manufacture both. C9 hodlers are a diamond-handed bunch.

author: Signal
content: I honestly think SCORE was your reward for buying ships early.  You got yield and payoff essentially for free.  I don’t think future ships need to be more expensive than past ships, because future ships will have to compete in SCREAM to earn anything back.  If in the grand scheme of things you only really paid for 60% of your ship, nobody should be upset by a ship costing 25% less than the OG SCORE ships later in the future.  I sincerely hope you get cheaper ships later.  Maybe just much more common and not as pretty but plenty functional for lower costs.  Then more expensive ships are just more rare and beautiful and cooler.  There’s plenty of ugly cheap cars that can drive as fast as pretty and more expensive ones.  That doesn’t break the automobile market.  Everyone wants a high five or special advantage for buying early.  That’s not always how the world works.  Sometimes your perk is just that you can say you were one of the first.  You bought a ship, hopefully because you liked it and wanted to play with it and liked the price.  That’s the end of the story.

content: I see your point, however I am leaning towards @Support Line | Star Atlas Mod 's line of thought, that releasing a $10k capital because of current market conditions and undercutting the previously set bar of $30k capital would be a bigger issue for community than ships that don't get sold. It's a tough one to deal with, but the market is so volatile that a pricing decision today is already incorrect tomorrow. Better stick with VWAP for relative value as that is a consistent yardstick that is already set.  Moreover, player manufacturing would take over at some point and I am assuming team would then only do sporadic special occasion type ships sales if at all.

author: [AEP] MoonMonger
content:
How long it takes to build a ship is not a simple question. The biggest factor is probably the assets owned by player trying to build one. A player who only has an X4 would have no ability to craft one vs a player with Tier 5 production structures with access to all required materials and capital ships constantly mining and hauling would be on the other end of the spectrum.

The teaming up to build a ship is interesting though. I can imagine some 3rd party add-ons managing the contributions of each player to a ship construction and ultimately the split of the profits.

author: [AEP] Moustacheride
content: Or all parts are sold at market price to the one player who builds it from components hopefully someone with manufacturing time reductions

author: [AEP] MoonMonger
content: So for example, I own a Tier 5 ship construction facility and I don't have the time or funds or desire to acquire all the resources and components for a Treearrow. I "contribute" my shipyard as the construction facility for the build into the Treearrow build smart contract. Other players fill all the other required components. Once the ship is sold, the profits are distributed among the contributors. Many details to work out... I am only theory crafting as to what a community build could look like.

author: odvb
content: I think this would be fine, if our ships had a fixed value that never fluctuated, but that is not the case. And insider trading is a problem, whether regulated or not, it's a problem. If people suspect that a player, or group of players, are playing the market using insider information, that puts a really negative light on the project as a whole, and will deter new players from entering into the space. But the comment that @odvb linked is sufficient for me for now, I will be monitoring the situation closely. I feel it already happened once before with the reward adjustments that were done on the Opal Jet and the Pearce x4, but we'll see.

author: [BSA] [EC] SquallMacthorioMUD
content: Towing is a good verb, when something (like a tow truck or a tug boat) is pulling another object/vehicle with a rope (or tractor beam). But if the ship is moving with its own engines, it's not really being towed. The more accurate term would be something like "supporting" if there's no pulling involved

content: XD its impossible to please everyone xD people spent 7 months asking for means of turning the downward trend of ATLAS and now people "complaining" (most of the time very respectfully) about recovering the ATLAS/USDC pair on ship prices or asking for increasing the ATLAS emissions. author: Quimera | JohnKeel ⪛⦿⫺ONI
content: Atlas emission curve havent changed since the start of SCORE because it follows a plan traced to create a new economy with specific mechanics and timings (i wont assume to know the full plan). Imagine you plan to create a new state somewhere, with a new coin, police, government structure administration etc. You cant just set the printer and gift millions of coins at once because they would have no value, yo need to set a slow stream of money to balance the economy

author: Quimera | JohnKeel ⪛⦿⫺ONI
content: About the ATLAS/USDC pairing, if SA keeps their compromise of not selling their ATLAS bags, increasing the costs in ATLAS will reduce circulating supply on markets while at same time continue rolling the emission plan to create the moving liquidity

author: ! ᚺEIM | VɪᴋɪɴɢᴇɴBᴇɴɴʏViking
content: Exactly, its a brand new company making the most ambitious game on blockchain if not even offchain. I dont get how eveneryone is so calm about it all, every release target was missed last year which is ok given the situation but still being lied to by saying the products is near complete when the development didnt even start is not something i appreciate from a startup tbh. Looking at the negative doesent really help no, but neither does pretending that everything is awesone. I still dont get why everyone is cool about it. As said above here the 426event is a celebration of another 426 promise being missed, new company or not i dont appreciate it, and im certainly not happy about it and still fail to understand why everyone is excited about another missed promise. Call me a fudder or whatever you wish but an old norse saying states a man who tells the truth no matter how grim the truth is, is a friend. A man who only speaks pleasantly, cannot be trusted. Well ive seen nothing but plesant talk here.. all i see is products being prioritized being marketed that isnt ment for gamers, after 7 months of nothing  i expected more simplified gaming elements. As of now what i know is scream is targeted for end of the year but they still dont have the tech available to make it. I guess i just want to be convinced  im wrong. Im not but i realize this foundation room is not the place for concerns lol

content:
I personally im not cool with it as you say im just unsurprised and not worried since my end game target is 5-8 years and I usually am more interested everytime I see new names in blue in discord than with the fomo events as you say, I just consider those as oportunities to spend time and check on people. 
Also I do think the foundation room is exactly the place to voice this concerns, a place I dont know any traditional projects have and the foundation room chat is somewhere were we can exchange opinions and different points of view. Im sorry if in anyway I gave the impression I was shutting you off, far from my intention, I think of the FR and FRC as a place where we can voice questions and concerns from the comunity and the other way around, share with the community what we learn here.

author: Quimera | JohnKeel ⪛⦿⫺ONI
content:
I am most possibly wrong, but I will share my perspective again because if things look dire for some rn I cant even imagine how they may look further down the line.
The emission "curve" for ATLAS was fixed for SCORE, emission per vwap is just the share pertaining to each ship on the global pool designed for SCORE in accordance to planed ship releases (maybe there is some flexibility there in terms of more or les ships actually released and duration of SCORE).
Those ATLAS can be considered as a reward for participating, as a simple return of investment or any other way of looking at it, a community of 216k people may offer and as such the community will always ask in this perspective what they deem best.
From my point of view the ATLAS emission pool pertaining to SCORE is just a small part of the plan to create the liquidity that in the end will sustain the SA economy. I dont know the full plan and none of its parts other than SCORE and what we may see in SCREAM and so I cant fully evaluate if its going well or not in the great scheme.
But something I am sure of is that it makes no sense to flood the market with liquidity when that liquidity have no use and that is why I personally have always appreciated the Team resolution during this past 7 months of not increasing the ATLAS rewards every time people have asked this.
Again, time will tell, maybe we get that million players by end of year and the ECON team decide the supply needs to be increased but in the perspective I hope this to be a fruitfull metaverse I appreciate some serious consideration on sistemic economy instead of short term price valuations.
(only referring to questions asking about increasing ATLAS rewards, about ATLAS/USDC repairing there are a few logic explanations but if people are already on edge I will keep those for myself)

author: Quimera | JohnKeel ⪛⦿⫺ONI
content: 120% speculation here but I cant let it go in advance of future complaints 426 months down the line, we still dont know how the tail of the curve will look like but we know SCORE wil "fade" to transition into SCREAM

author: [AEP] MoonMonger
content:
Yield per $ of VWAP is the same for all ships. Repegging ATLAS cost to USDC VWAP for unsold dev tranches does not change that. What it changes is that ATMTA would get the fair value in ATLAS IF those ships are sold. We should be grateful that this was not done before, as the expectation that it WOULD be done was set from the beginning.

The other number to keep in mind is Yield on Cost. The cheaper you can buy a ship from VWAP, the higher your YoC would be. So yes, if there is no discount, you have lower yield. However, the net effect here so far is that ATLAS is pumping increasing our yield in USDC terms AND our asset value is increasing.

author: shibby
content: Well the problem is it was never fine in the first place, but now i just learned the whole event to celebrate the team is being on time has producs being delayed which is not cool at all.. and whats being delayed? Showroom and novel, aparently it has a lower priority than DAO, Markets and referals.   Whats the issue? MARKETS - Team will reset the Atlas Price of ships to UPDATE it to the current USD price of Atlas.  DAO - The team will stake their majority of the Polis, sucking up majority of rewardpool from the community, to sell to us at a later stage when they "give away" their polis majority.   REFERALS - They want us, the community to bring in more people as they have not yet delivered any gameplay to have their marketing capaign to work efficiently bringing in new participants..   Personally i already refered my friends and they are down 70% and dont trust crypto or SA at all due to all the false promises. We were told that the Trailer released in september 21(maybe i got wrong month) were actual gameplay and a team member was flying and landing the opod. author: CheckmateONI
content: markets:  we'll simply stop enjoying a massive discount after the whole market's crashed since inception for the atlas side of markets in the devs's tranches. Theyve provably missed out on several tens of millions worth by both not adjusting and holding the earned atlas from selling all the while we've been able to enjoy the benefit of 80% discounted ship buying opps.  The DAO:  the team wont minmax for yield, nor is that the main objective. they will need to stake to gain VP (voting power) to have the majority say in the decisions on where the game goes into its development for the next coming years before all is built out and the majority is handed over in ways of paid out Polis from the DAO staking and missions ingame that only goes to the community.  Referrals:  simply working as an incentivisor to onboard people by word of mouth while SA's getting more of its game brought out.  The entire market's been down since StarAtlas's first inception with the likes of SCORE in december, getting family to buy BTC in that same time obviously also would get some sceptisism now  Lets await which releases the #426Live event brings us, as it will be a celebration of a massive leap forward in what is and will become StarAtlas

author: Ethan
content:
I am most interested in understanding how ships are going to be valued in SCREAM and the “end game” - something we simply don’t have the data to compute yet. I accept that it’ll probably be a few years before that becomes clear. But the question that I’ll be patiently awaiting the answer to is - absent of SCORE, what rationale exists for spending $100,000 on a C11? 

People spend $1000s on ships in Star Citizen for nothing more than the fun of it and bragging rights, and I’ve played other “pay to win” games where people drop $100,000 on assets that can not be resold, but those people are the rare exception and certainly not the rule.

author: Lysimach | Make Polis Great [STAR]
content: Markets - We purposefully took on the blow on $atlas pairings during the down trend so our community could get ships at a “discount”. We’ve said before that we would readjust so I don’t think this is a huge surprise to many people. I’m personally really happy that core community members were able to get discounted ships for so long.   DAO - We’ve also always have said that we’d be staking our POLIS and that the rewards for staking weren’t supposed to be crazy or anything like that.  Referral Program - I hear you here and there’s definitely a reason we’re launching this close to actual releases. I’ve referred my friends too who are down, every market is down so they understand, though.  We love you guys and always always always appreciate the support. I promise you that we’re always keeping in mind our community for all the decisions we make.  Happy to talk in a voice chat as well if you’d want that. Much love Benny

author: Signal
content: Well the problem is it was never fine in the first place, but now i just learned the whole event to celebrate the team is being on time has producs being delayed which is not cool at all.. and whats being delayed? Showroom and novel, aparently it has a lower priority than DAO, Markets and referals.   Whats the issue? MARKETS - Team will reset the Atlas Price of ships to UPDATE it to the current USD price of Atlas.  DAO - The team will stake their majority of the Polis, sucking up majority of rewardpool from the community, to sell to us at a later stage when they "give away" their polis majority.   REFERALS - They want us, the community to bring in more people as they have not yet delivered any gameplay to have their marketing capaign to work efficiently bringing in new participants..   Personally i already refered my friends and they are down 70% and dont trust crypto or SA at all due to all the false promises.  So delayed is the Showroom, some form of small Gameplay that the team does not monetize from. And the Graphics novel, where also they dont monetize from it.  Thats my issue, and this whole 426 thing on top of it..... i cant tell you how much it just makes me sad about the whole situation..  We were told that the Trailer released in september 21(maybe i got wrong month) were actual gameplay and a team member was flying and landing the opod. If the showroom was complete to that level of detail with flight controls and animations 10 months ago, whats taking so long with the showroom?   I have been absent from the FR for a long time for sure but ive never been ok with this stuff, just keeping to myself in hopes things would get better

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content: Pretty sure I heard Bunthius stating the Showroom was due in October. If you've been following the weekly updates, as I've already told you, there isn't much left on their "To-Do List" about the Showroom.  The "October" announcement came weeks well before both COPA and 426 (though I'm happy to be corrected here). As far as the Novel and POLIS DAO, I am interested in neither so I can't give you useful insight.  Now, the team has seen some slippage over its targets but heavy work has only begun this year and they stated two major releases would be due, with the Showroom being one of them and the DAO being the other one (again, do correct me if I'm wrong). They didn't say the exact month, though.  So, in my view. My opinion alone, Automata owes* me this. Get the two major releases out. If they come out? Great. It's business as usual and we'll keep it that way until the next two major releases or so. If they do not? Time for me (not them) to review my investments and act (if I already have an exit strategy) or plan (if I don't) accordingly. Worst case scenario, it's BDA and RTB. Either way, it's in my hands to take action.  I do like the game vision though and it's why I've invested in it. But don't get me wrong here. While I like Wagner, Santi, etc, it's more of a "countries don't have friends, only interests" standpoint for me. SA takes care of mine, I'll take care of theirs.   *Figure of speech really. They owe me nothing but I expect them not to disappoint. I have risk management strats in place but ultimately I am fully aware that the thousands I have invested can amount to absolute zero tomorrow. It's just the way it is with crypto.  EDIT ↓ What Funcracker said

author: [AEP] Moustacheride
content: Above is response from visualjc

author: [AEP] Moustacheride
content:
And below:

Wow, these are great questions; and yes, these are incredibly serious challenges for any MMO to solve.

1. Lets say 30k players for an faction and everyone starts at the same station/location, how will that work?
This question ultimately comes down to Density; which poses three problems: 1) the quadratic growth rate of network traffic, 2) the polygon count, 3) the tremendous visual 'noise' of having 30k things moving around you on a screen.

1. To limit network traffic we are working on spatially rate limited network traffic and predictive client side modeling. Basically, the farther away the fleet is from you, the less network traffic we are going to send you for that fleet; even to the point where we roll up a bunch of fleets and we send their 'location' as one X,Y, then the client makes some predictive modeling to pull the ships apart. 
- Because space is so big, we can be pretty aggressive with this algorithm.
- Danny probably flagged me as I am personally working on the server side quad tree culling algorithm for this.

2. For polygon and visual noise, when a player is zoomed out, we are working on something similar, where fleets, and fleets of fleets simply become icons on a bigger map.  Again, rolling up individual information to make it more understandable.

So, if 30k, players all start at the same time, we would start them all in some relative distance from the same 'station', but programmatically spread apart so that we can limit each player to receiving ~300-400 fleet updates per second.  You might not get updates for fleets that are 100k+ Kilometers from you for 30 seconds or more, while receiving the fleets that 10k Kilo's or less every 0.5 seconds.

content:
Hey guys, an idea inspired by @GoldenLover | BubblePop's idea on buying ships with dao funds for free2play.

What do you think about buying ships with dao funds to reward active players?

Something like a weekly lottery for a ship worth, say 5k (or multiple cheaper ships), and then a grand prize once each month (a ship worth 10k or whatever).

Entry requirements could be something like 5 hours or 10 hours of gameplay each week (or through minimum LP earned).

It would incentivize activity, provide a chance for players that can't afford ships to earn ships, continuously buy new ships, etc.

author: Oscar GF | Starke FinanceUstur
content: Hey guys, an idea inspired by @GoldenLover | BubblePop's idea on buying ships with dao funds for free2play.  What do you think about buying ships with dao funds to reward active players?  Something like a weekly lottery for a ship worth, say 5k (or multiple cheaper ships), and then a grand prize once each month (a ship worth 10k or whatever).  Entry requirements could be something like 5 hours or 10 hours of gameplay each week (or through minimum LP earned).  It would incentivize activity, provide a chance for players that can't afford ships to earn ships, continuously buy new ships, etc.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: Thanks! I was really more curious as to if a Faction or Regional DAOs boarders can be expanded, thus I needed to establish a baseline somewhere.   For example, if Regional DAO 1 Borders Regional DAO 7, can Regional DAO 1 expand its border and claim Regional DAO 7's territory, either through politics or conflict?   The true question is how will political boundaries and territorial boundaries ebb and flow - its almost as if there is a political map on top of the game map. Elite Dangerous players know you can change map view settings bases on political influence as opposed to who actually owns a system, so the concept is already there and understood

author: [HEIM] Drakkainen
content: I would guess that whatever was defined as MRZ could not become HRZ later. Because the rules of the zones are so different, i.e. permanent loss, I imagine that having invested into land with one set of rules it would be unfair for them to change later. Also a known benefit of higher risk zones is rarer minerals and those can't just appear or disappear because a zone changed risk level.  That being said, I believe zones will expand over time as we get more players, just not get recategorized into different risk levels. Just my speculation on it...

author: [AEP] Kavorka
content: The DAO could create specific rules prohibiting a proposal that involves a conflict of interest (and describing how potential conflicts can be resolved)--the DAO will be governed not just by code but also by its own set of laws (with the Star Atlas Constitution as the highest law).  But perhaps a sufficient approach will be built into the PIP drafting / discussion / revision process, by affording the community with enough time and opportunity to review, comment, and research other opportunities.  This way, the DAO ensures it gets arm's length terms on all proposals, so even if a proposal originates from an interested party, the terms are reasonably in line with what a competing third party would provide the DAO.  The DAO also could require disclosure where a proposer has personal or financial interest in the proposal.  All different approaches the DAO could consider / combine for its purposes.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: To your point about multi-dacs moving POLIS around the factions DAOs, I think there is sufficient mechanics in place combined with enough decentralization that it would be difficult for a multi-faction group to have any serious advantage in that regard  The multi-faction DAC would essentially have to hold 3x the amount of POLIS compared to a DAC in a single faction to be competetive in PVP (assuming same duration of locks)   Additionally, if a multi-fac moves their POLIS around too often, it would leave their retreating PIP positions vulnerable to counter proposals from withing their originating faction

content: Playing both sides of the fence will always be controversial, especial with real $$$ at stake. If you don't find it appropriate I can understand that and reason with that. just as much as I dont find it appropriate that you wish I be bound to speak only in pure neutrality with no expression of opinion or at the very least wish that i slink off to a anonymous account to divulge my personal sentiments.

author: [GIA]Strictz9
content: I’m still a bit new to Star Atlas though I love the vision.  What is the vision for the scope of the universe?  I know games like No Man’s Sky made the mistake of choosing quantity over quality, but obviously making things too confined has drawbacks too.  Is the vision to make a base star system then expanded things out?  I’m wondering if things will resemble Star Citizen’s model where they continuously expanding their universe.  I really like their model except for the part where it took 10 years to get there.  If Star Atlas tries to take that long I’m sure competition will blow passed them eventually.  But that’s one thing I’ve learned to value in gamefi, if the game isn’t fun to play without the aspect of making money involved then it won’t survive the longterm.  I’m very interested in seeing how much fun Scream is to play when it launches.

author: [GIA]Strictz9
content: I listened to the debate.  I think you did very well Michael keeping your poise and professionalism.  Illuvium and Star Atlas have two very different visions and I’m glad that was brought out.  Gamefi is still in the atari era for the most part transitioning into the NES era.  Illuvium’s vision is to build a game with the existing infrastructure available right now while Star Atlas’s focus seems to be on building a game of what is envisioned to be possible in this space, not just our current limitations.  Kieran did have one very valid concern in my mind and that is how do we balance rewarding early adopters while still protecting the gamer that joins this project 5 years from now?  Early adopters should absolutely reap a reward for being here early and being an early believer in the vision, but that shouldn’t mean we all turn into whales that just bully around newbies down the road.  The guilds will help mitigate that I think.  But what are the ideas behind protecting that like “sacred path” where a gamer can join 5 years from now with a $50 investment and still have a rewarding and fun experience, and if they are willing to grind it out can really make a good return on their time and money as they work their way up the ladder in the game?  In short how do you give new adopters years from now a rewarding experience while still rewarding early adopters and not let this whole situation descend into newbies down the road essentially being used as exit liquidity for early adopters?

Kieran did have one very valid concern in my mind and that is how do we balance rewarding early adopters while still protecting the gamer that joins this project 5 years from now? Early adopters should absolutely reap a reward for being here early and being an early believer in the vision, but that shouldn’t mean we all turn into whales that just bully around newbies down the road. The guilds will help mitigate that I think. But what are the ideas behind protecting that like “sacred path” where a gamer can join 5 years from now with a $50 investment and still have a rewarding and fun experience, and if they are willing to grind it out can really make a good return on their time and money as they work their way up the ladder in the game? In short how do you give new adopters years from now a rewarding experience while still rewarding early adopters and not let this whole situation descend into newbies down the road essentially being used as exit liquidity for early adopters?

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content:
I'm all for capitalism. But more importantly, I support the Japanese concept of Ikigai. It's a representation of balance:
Passion
Capability
Value to the world
Personal Finance

Ikigai represents a 'sense of purpose.'  https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ikigai

Making money is the least important of those attributes. Is it a necessity? Yes. But should it be our driving force? No. Anyone driven purely by 'getting rich' is not pursuing the appropriate path. And for someone charading as an advocate for our space, I find inherent conflict in his motivators.

author: Ethan
content:
@[AEP] Mandalorian (Djin Djarin) regarding your question about balance. Here's my understanding based on my own MMO experience. 

Fast forward 5 years. SCREAM has been on the market for 4 years and I finally convince my buddy (Bob) to come play. He pays $20 for a ship and joins Aephia (the best guild, ofc). He can fly around the safe zone, complete missions, and interact with the game in fun and engaging ways without risking the destruction of his assets. If he wants to venture into the medium risk zone, he can. But he's smart and slightly risk averse, so he makes sure to coordinate with his guild mates to ensure that if they run into a pesky group of pirates, they can provide mutual defense. Strength in numbers. Now he's been playing for a few months, progressed his character, upgraded his ship, bought some land (in a moderately safe area of the medium risk zone "controlled" by his faction/guild). He's bought another $20 ship and is willing to risk it in the high risk zone. Once again, he partners with his guild mates to go on a high risk mission where he plays a role (even if its a small one) in defeating some much more experienced players with much larger and more expensive ships. For his role in helping win the battle, he gets a portion of the rewards. 

Now let's imagine Joe. Joe is crazy. Joe buys a $20 ship, doesn't join a guild, flies right into the HRZ and starts pestering a C11. Joes (rightfully) gets blown to bits and loses his ship. The game is not unbalanced. Joe is just an idiot. (No offense if your name is Joe)

author: Ethan
content: To add to this, it feels like SCREAM and future modules are going to be fairly time and labor intensive (meaning that factions and guilds) are going to rely heavily on 1000s of players with their sub $1000 ships to accomplish their objectives. And those players are going to want to join the guilds and factions that reward them the most for their contributions.

author: [BSA] [EC] SquallMacthorioMUD
content: I dont think this was the thought behind the question. From my understanding it was fearing it'd become a pay 2 win scenario, well say Greg buys 100k ship and can easily dominate guys like Joe and Bob, ragardless of their risk assessment from Day 1. Creating a P2W scenario. Im not sure about the mechanics behind it all, if there is something that will prevent this to some degree through playtime/skill system where @《VΛ》Bullet put some good thoughts.  Personally i like games where people start from scratch, you cant be mad at someone for grinding their way to the top in games.

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content:
So many facets to this question 

Let’s pick one:
If you are a lvl 5 Dwarven Paladin in World of Warcraft, and you venture into Contested territory where a lvl 60 Orc Shaman (bought as insta level-60, no grind) makes short work of you.. Is that:

 Unbalanced; the dwarf was new and had no chance against this other person who spent the same amount of time in-game

 Balanced; the dwarf took a risk, and ventured into MRZ space without being prepared / just unlucky

 Something else, let me know.. 

Would it change if the Orc Shaman leveled from 1-60 instead?

The dwarf could have spent more time in the SZ perhaps, or.. find some friends from his guild to venture with him into the MRZ 

Curious about any thoughts on this.

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content: Dang.. It’s been too long..

author: Xap✨ [STAR]
content: P2W come into play if there is high reward for someone to pay and win against smaller players. If it is not worth it then it is not P2W. If high level account wants to get benefit for their investment they have to go against high value targets. A smaller player will not attempt those adventures until they are at the right level.

author: ! ᚺEIM | VɪᴋɪɴɢᴇɴBᴇɴɴʏViking
content:
Naturally a PX4 vs C11 the balance is natural and dont need to be explained. That's not really the issue i see tho.

Imagine you wrecked your ass in your chair grinding your way through the game for several months, aquire yourself a ballad and go to battle, assuming its a battle of the ballads, you win, fair and square. The guy you just wrecked is a rich feller with a low temper, he buys another ballad, goes straight back and wrecks you. 

This happens in alot of games and makes them unplayable. What if a young low tempered billionaire dickhead decides he doesent like Aephia and spends million on assets just to harass you? Sure its very unlikley but regardless SA is what SA is and there no way you cant limit ship sales naturally.

Edited a few key letters missing giving the sentence a different meaning xD

author: Xap✨ [STAR]
content: That is why on day one of Scream I may not use Atlas to upgrade hanger to bring in larger ships. It may not be beneficial. Perhaps focusing on strategizing and playing to gain XP and LP would be the focus. Once a plan start to work then strategically apply ATLAS to bring in larger ships if gives advantage. I do not expect value from C11 on day one.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: If those tasks are entertaining, there should be no problem. If they are just play-to-earn busy tasks to support an economy, it might be a problem. You would get involvement where the IRL value goes farther and p2e makes sense, but you won't attract and keep players who are looking for actual entertainment.

reply.preview: If those tasks are entertaining, there should be no problem. If they are just play-to-earn busy tasks to support an economy, it might be a problem. You would get involvement where the IRL value goes farther and p2e makes sense, but you won't attract and keep players who are looking for actual entertainment.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: That said, our audience is larger than the traditional gamer cohort. The primary P2E community largely comes from emerging markets. So their motivations might not be aligned with having fun, but more focused on the ability to earn. So, to that end, it might actually make sense to build in mechanics that are more supply chain oriented and repetitive, but easy for everyone to do.

author: JayWalk
content: "low tempered billionaires" will be great for the overall-value of the assets in SA. Every $ that gets injected to the system lifts all participants, and just like in life, smaller players economically will need to fight harder to climb the ladder. I believe that this is good, skill and coordination of the small-guy will have the opportunity to beat the rich, and in the end Skills and Smarts will be rewarded.

content: The staking lockers carry different voting multipliers, but do they have different emission rates as well?

author: [COEX] KapganONI
content: They do not. It is a single pool. Your voting weight determines your proportional share of daily emissions. So, your totalPVP = POLISamt * votingMultiplier. Your dailyEmissions = userTotalPVP / ecosystemTotalPVP

author: [AEP] Bodhitree

author: odvb
content: They are able to change the source code of your browser. (Might not have the perfect verbiage) effectively they delete your phantom wallet and replace it with a fake replica thats identical in every way from a ui standpoint. But the tx is changed under the hood and you sign a bad tx either through the extension or on the ledger
